DEPARTMENT F46 LAW AND MOTION RULINGS

Call the Courtroom Assistant at 818-576-8430 prior to 4:30 p.m. on the date prior to the hearing, or inform the Courtroom Assistant upon checking in.

Case Number: 21CHCV00539    Hearing Date: March 2, 2026    Dept: F46

Dept. F-46

Hearing Date: March 2, 2026

Case Number: 21CHCV00539

 

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT

 

Motion filed on December 12, 2025.

MOVING PARTY: Defendant Rayne Dealership Corporation.

RESPONDING PARTY: No opposition filed.

NOTICE: OK.

RELIEF REQUESTED: Defendant Rayne Dealership Corporation (Defendant) seeks leave to file an amended cross-complaint.

RULING: Denied without prejudice.

 

BACKGROUND

 

On July 21, 2021, Daniel S. Wallis and Diane L. Wallis (collectively, Plaintiffs) filed a complaint against Culligan International Company and Rayne Water Conditioning, Inc.

 

On July 29, 2022, Plaintiffs filed a first amended complaint for breach of contract and negligence arising from a water damage incident on July 15, 2020.

 

On April 9, 2024, Plaintiffs amended the complaint to substitute Rayne Dealership Corporation as DOE 1.

 

On May 17, 2024, Defendant Rayne Dealership Corporation filed its Answer. On August 9, 2024, Defendant filed a cross-complaint against Hydronix Water Technology.

 

On September 24, 2025, Defendant filed a motion for leave to file a cross-complaint. The Court denied that motion without prejudice on November 14, 2025, because the moving papers incorrectly identified the party as Rayne Corporation and failed to attach a proposed cross-complaint.

 

On December 17, 2025, Defendant filed the current motion for leave to file an amended cross-complaint supported by a memorandum of points and authorities and the declaration of Linwood Warren Jr.

 

ANALYSIS

 

Procedural Requirements

 

A party shall obtain leave of court to file any cross-complaint except one filed before or at the same time as the answer to the complaint. (Code Civ. Proc., § 428.50, subd. (c)). Leave may be granted in the interest of justice at any time during the course of the action. (Ibid.). A trial court shall grant leave to file a compulsory cross-complaint if the party who failed to plead the cause acted in good faith. (Code Civ. Proc., § 426.50).

 

Here, Defendant seeks leave to file a cross-complaint after it already filed its Answer on May 17, 2024. (Warren Decl. ¶ 4). Defendant asserts that it acted in good faith because it was ignorant of the facts that make the proposed cross-defendants liable at the time it filed its Answer. Defendant states that further investigation after serving the Answer revealed that proposed cross-defendants share liability for the damages. (Warren Decl. ¶ 5). Because the Answer has been filed, Defendant must obtain leave of court to proceed. (Code Civ. Proc., § 428.50, subd. (c)).

 

Sufficiency of the Record

 

The moving papers must contain a notice, declaration of counsel, points and authorities, and a copy of the proposed cross-complaint. (Weil and Brown, Cal. Prac. Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2025) ¶ 6:561).

 

Here, Defendant moves for leave to file an amended cross-complaint. While the motion includes a declaration and a memorandum of points and authorities, it does not include a copy of the proposed amended cross-complaint. Based on the record provided, the Court cannot evaluate whether the proposed causes of action are compulsory or permissive, nor if the claims arise out of the same transaction or occurrence as proposed.

 

CONCLUSION

 

The Motion for Leave to File Amended Cross-Complaint filed by Defendant Rayne Dealership Corporation is denied without prejudice.
